fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Texas Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Texas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity texas, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to texas, speech-language pathologist compact texas, texas speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists Program
board.parent_dept: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice speech-language pathology in Texas. Requires a master's degree, Praxis exam, and completion of a supervised Clinical Fellowship (CF).

license_types.title: SLP Intern License
license_types.description: For Clinical Fellows completing their post-graduate supervised experience. Valid during the CF period under an ASHA-certified mentor.

steps.title: Meet Education Requirements
steps.description: You must have a master’s degree or doctoral degree in speech-language pathology from an accredited educational institution.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Experience
steps.description: You must complete a supervised clinical practicum and a supervised postgraduate professional employment experience (Clinical Fellowship).

steps.title: Pass the Examination
steps.description: Pass the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology and apply for licensure with the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulations.

documents.detail: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.
CF supervisor attestation or ASHA CCC-SLP certificate.
Completed via the TDLR FAST Pass system.

telepractice_text: Texas permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. The practitioner must hold a valid Texas SLP license regardless of where they are physically located. Services delivered to patients in Texas require Texas licensure.
provisional_text: Texas issues an SLP Intern License for Clinical Fellows. This allows supervised practice during the CF period. The intern must be directly supervised by an ASHA-certified SLP holding a valid Texas license.
reciprocity_text: Texas does not have formal reciprocity agreements. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for a new Texas license, submit proof of their master's degree, Praxis score, and completed Clinical Fellowship. If you hold current ASHA CCC-SLP certification, this streamlines the process as it demonstrates you have met national standards.

faqs.question: How much does a Texas SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application fee is $80, paid to TDLR. You must also pay approximately $37 for the fingerprint-based background check, bringing the total to about $117.

faqs.question: Does Texas require the Praxis exam for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes. All initial applicants must pass the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology (test code 5331) with a score of 162 or higher.

faqs.question: Can I practice SLP via telepractice in Texas?
faqs.answer: Yes. Texas permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. You must hold a valid Texas SLP license to provide services to patients located in Texas, regardless of where you are physically located.

faqs.question: Does Texas have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Texas participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Texas without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Texas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Texas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Texas does not have formal reciprocity agreements. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for a new Texas license, submit proof of their master's degree, Praxis score, and completed Clinical Fellowship. If you hold current ASHA CCC-SLP certification, this streamlines the process as it demonstrates you have met national standards.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Texas
hero.dek: Texas Speech-Language Pathologist License requires a master's degree in speech-language pathology, passing the Praxis exam, and completing a supervised clinical practicum.
